findings.msg: Multiple cards embed instructor answer-key tells ("she flagged this as exam-important", L2-C017 "she said this is likely on the exam", L2-P011/P012 "Confirmed — answer B/C"), showing the deck is built from one past exam's key rather than the curriculum.
findings.fix: Strip the meta annotations and justify inclusion by course learning objectives, not by what one exam asked.

findings.msg: L1-P012 and L1-C008 are pinned to a specific artifact ("the HW1 gel", "resolves HW1 Q6"), indicating reverse-engineering from one homework rather than the general gel-reading concept.
findings.fix: Rewrite as a generic gel-interpretation problem not tied to HW1.

findings.msg: Only one tag (nucleotide-chemistry) despite covering sugar/phosphate/base structure.
findings.fix: Add dna-structure as a second tag.

findings.msg: Single tag (foundations); Occam's-razor test-taking card is under-indexed.
findings.fix: Add a second tag (e.g. compare-contrast) or reclassify.

findings.msg: L1-P012 and L1-P020 are the same gel item — Band 1 near wells / Band 2 farther / 'which statement is NOT consistent' — differing only in which band the trap option names.
findings.fix: Cut one, or repurpose it to test a different gel skill (e.g. estimating fragment size vs a ladder).

findings.msg: L1-C009 and L1-P007 both ask the identical question 'which Southern step is not needed in a Northern' with the same answer (restriction digestion).
findings.fix: Drop L1-P007 or reframe it around the alkali-denaturation step instead.

findings.msg: "Topoisomerases (a.k.a. gyrases)" is wrong—gyrase is one specific bacterial type II topoisomerase, not a synonym for the whole class (type I cut one strand, type II both).
findings.fix: Drop the "a.k.a. gyrases" equivalence; note gyrase is the bacterial type II example.

findings.msg: Telomerase discovery is dated 1987, but Greider & Blackburn identified telomerase activity in 1984 (published 1985 in Cell); telomere repeats were characterized earlier still.
findings.fix: Change discovery year to 1985.

findings.msg: Pauling's 1949 sickle-cell work used Tiselius moving-boundary (free-solution) electrophoresis, not gel electrophoresis, which was introduced later (Smithies, starch gel, 1955).
findings.fix: Say "electrophoresis (moving-boundary)," not "gel electrophoresis."

findings.msg: Carothers 1913 grasshopper independent assortment (plus Wilson 1895 L1-R034, Sutton/Boveri L1-R035) is chromosome-theory/Mendelian history, not DNA structure/replication/transcription.
findings.fix: Move these to a transmission-genetics unit or drop for Unit 1.

findings.msg: An Occam's-razor "how to pick the best multiple-choice answer" card teaches exam strategy, not molecular genetics.
findings.fix: Remove; it's untied to any course concept.

findings.msg: Darwin's "descent with modification" is an evolution/variation aside with no molecular-genetics payload for this unit.
findings.fix: Cut or fold into a single foundations card.

findings.msg: Single foundations tag; same on L1-R002 and L1-R004 (all <2).
findings.fix: Add a second relevant tag (e.g. who-what-when/compare-contrast) or confirm these are irreducibly single-domain.

findings.msg: Tagged 'telomere' but the question is about prokaryotic replication (origins, ligase, Okazaki) — no telomere content.
findings.fix: Replace telomere with leading-lagging or primer-removal.

findings.msg: L2-C014 and L2-P011 are the same card — 'why is reactivating telomerase dangerous → cancer risk' — with identical reasoning.
findings.fix: Keep the concept card; delete the problem card or make it a numeric ~80-90% recall.

findings.msg: L2-C012 and L2-P007 both ask how the extended G-overhang becomes double-stranded, both answering 'next cell cycle as Okazaki fragments.'
findings.fix: Merge into one card.

findings.msg: L1 has ~15 pure who/when/date history cards (Miescher, Wilson, Sutton/Boveri, Carothers, Pauling, Southern, Astbury, plus dated Griffith/Avery/Hershey-Chase repeats), heavy relative to typical exam weight on mechanism over trivia.
findings.fix: Consolidate the date-recall cards into a couple of timeline cards and shift weight toward mechanism/interpretation.

findings.msg: Thermus aquaticus was described by Brock & Freeze in 1969 (Yellowstone sampling mid-1960s); "1965" for the organism is imprecise.
findings.fix: Attribute 1969 to the T. aquaticus description, or drop the specific year.

findings.msg: The "Brock, Yellowstone, 1965" collection detail for Taq is biographical trivia beyond the thermostability point that matters.
findings.fix: Keep the thermostable-from-Thermus-aquaticus fact; drop the collector/year.

findings.msg: The hypothetical archaeal "extra bases Y and Z that pair only with each other" is an invented scenario more puzzle-like than curricular.
findings.fix: Verify it reflects taught material; otherwise flag as enrichment, not core.

findings.msg: Tagged 'calculation' but the card is pure recall of B-form dimensions with no computation (L1-R012 similarly mislabels 'calculation' for rule-statement).
findings.fix: Drop calculation; keep dna-structure.

findings.msg: L1-R014 and L2-R001 both define 'semiconservative'; L2-R001 supersedes it by also listing the rejected models.
findings.fix: If cross-lecture repetition is intended keep both; otherwise retire L1-R014.

findings.msg: L1-P010 and L2-P015 are the same problem type (count phosphodiester + H-bonds in a duplex) with different sequences.
findings.fix: Fine as spaced practice; flag only if trimming.

findings.msg: L2-R027 and L2-P012 both resolve to 'Taq chosen because thermostable / survives 95°C.'
findings.fix: Acceptable recall/problem pairing; drop one if tightening.

findings.msg: Unit 1 nominally includes transcription, but the deck has zero transcription cards (only DNA structure, hereditary-material history, techniques, and replication).
findings.fix: Add transcription cards or note the unit scope excludes it.

findings.msg: Cross-cutting skill tags (directionality, mechanism, who-what-when, compare-contrast, figure-interpretation, exceptions, calculation) are applied consistently across problem/concept cards; no systemic omission for this lens.
